claim: Deletion of αvβ8 does not result in a spontaneous inflammatory phenotype.

result.claim: Deletion of αvβ8 does not result in a spontaneous inflammatory phenotype.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper ID 17997584, titled "Integrin αvβ8-Mediated TGF-β Activation by Effector Regulatory T Cells Is Essential for Suppression of T-Cell-Mediated Inflammation." Specifically, proposition prop_2759 and chunk_350 from this paper state: "Treg-cell-specific deletion of integrin αvβ8 did not result in a spontaneous inflammatory phenotype." This directly confirms the claim. No refuting evidence was found.

evidence.text: Genetic ablation of cyclooxygenases (COX) in Braf(V600E) mouse melanoma cells renders them susceptible to immune control.
evidence.source: Cyclooxygenase-Dependent Tumor Growth through Evasion of Immunity

evidence.text: Autospecific CD4(+) T cells showed increased Th2 cytokine responses in the absence of IL-12.
evidence.source: Dual role of the IL-12/IFN-gamma axis in the development of autoimmune myocarditis: induction by IL-12 and protection by IFN-gamma.

evidence.text: Mice lacking junctional adhesion molecule A (JAM-A, encoded by F11r) exhibit elevated colonic lymphocyte numbers.
evidence.source: Compromised intestinal epithelial barrier induces adaptive immune compensation that protects from colitis.

evidence.text: The presence of an autoimmune disease-associated IL2RA haplotype correlates with diminished IL-2 responsiveness in Ag-experienced CD4(+) T cells, as measured by phosphorylation of STAT5a.
evidence.source: Type 1 diabetes-associated IL2RA variation lowers IL-2 signaling and contributes to diminished CD4+CD25+ regulatory T cell function.

evidence.text: Alpha(+)-thalassaemia is a heritable haemoglobinopathy.
evidence.source: Increased Microerythrocyte Count in Homozygous α+-Thalassaemia Contributes to Protection against Severe Malarial Anaemia

evidence.text: Th1-type cytokine production was decreased in the gp130FXXQ/FXXQ immune system.
evidence.source: Dissection of signaling cascades through gp130 in vivo: reciprocal roles for STAT3- and SHP2-mediated signals in immune responses.

evidence.text: IFN-gamma receptor-deficient mice (IFN-gammaR(-/-)) on a BALB/c background immunized with a peptide derived from cardiac alpha-myosin heavy chain develop severe myocarditis.
evidence.source: Lethal autoimmune myocarditis in interferon-gamma receptor-deficient mice: enhanced disease severity by impaired inducible nitric oxide synthase induction.

evidence.text: Ly49Q mediated rapid neutrophil polarization and tissue infiltration in an ITIM-domain-dependent manner in the presence of inflammatory stimuli.
evidence.source: The Ly49Q receptor plays a crucial role in neutrophil polarization and migration by regulating raft trafficking.

evidence.text: Interferon-gamma (IFN-gamma) is a cytokine involved in the regulation of inflammatory responses in autoimmune diseases.
evidence.source: Lethal autoimmune myocarditis in interferon-gamma receptor-deficient mice: enhanced disease severity by impaired inducible nitric oxide synthase induction.

evidence.text: Acute infection with Listeria monocytogenes elicits inflammatory conditions.
evidence.source: Subpopulations of mouse blood monocytes differ in maturation stage and inflammatory response.

evidence.text: Small-vessel vasculitis (SVV) is a chronic autoinflammatory condition.
evidence.source: Netting neutrophils in autoimmune small-vessel vasculitis

evidence.text: Autospecific CD4(+) T cells proliferated poorly in the absence of IL-12.
evidence.source: Dual role of the IL-12/IFN-gamma axis in the development of autoimmune myocarditis: induction by IL-12 and protection by IFN-gamma.

evidence.text: Treg-cell-specific deletion of integrin αvβ8 did not result in a spontaneous inflammatory phenotype.
evidence.source: Integrin αvβ8-Mediated TGF-β Activation by Effector Regulatory T Cells Is Essential for Suppression of T-Cell-Mediated Inflammation

evidence.text: Treg cells lacking expression of integrin αvβ8 were unable to suppress pathogenic T cell responses during active inflammation.
evidence.source: Integrin αvβ8-Mediated TGF-β Activation by Effector Regulatory T Cells Is Essential for Suppression of T-Cell-Mediated Inflammation

evidence.text: There was a significant increase in immature Ly-6C(high) monocytes under inflammatory conditions elicited either by acute infection with Listeria monocytogenes or chronic infection with Leishmania major.
evidence.source: Subpopulations of mouse blood monocytes differ in maturation stage and inflammatory response.

evidence.text: IL-4 and IL-13 abrogated autophagy in murine and human macrophages.
evidence.source: T helper 2 cytokines inhibit autophagic control of intracellular Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

evidence.text: Small-vessel vasculitis (SVV) is linked to antineutrophil cytoplasm autoantibodies (ANCAs).
evidence.source: Netting neutrophils in autoimmune small-vessel vasculitis

evidence.text: Expansion of host macrophages prevented the development of alveolar proteinosis in mice transplanted with GM-Csf-receptor-deficient progenitors.
evidence.source: Tissue-resident macrophages self-maintain locally throughout adult life with minimal contribution from circulating monocytes.

evidence.text: CD4(+)CD25(+) regulatory T cells (Tregs) from individuals with a range of human autoimmune diseases are deficient in their ability to control autologous proinflammatory responses when compared with nondiseased, control individuals.
evidence.source: Type 1 diabetes-associated IL2RA variation lowers IL-2 signaling and contributes to diminished CD4+CD25+ regulatory T cell function.

evidence.text: N-cadherin–specific antibodies reduced serum interleukin-8 (IL-8) secretion.
evidence.source: Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin inhibits prostate cancer growth, metastasis and castration resistance
